title: Quantization & Mixed Precision
summary: Reduce numerical precision of weights and activations (float32 to int8/int4) for faster inference and smaller model size. Post-training quantization requires no retraining. Quantization-aware training fine-tunes with simulated low precision. Mixed precision: float16 forward/backward, float32 accumulation.
key_insights: Post-training quantization (PTQ): quantize after training, no retraining needed. Easy but some accuracy loss.
Quantization-aware training (QAT): simulate quantization during training. Better accuracy, requires retraining.
Mixed precision training: float16 for most ops (2x memory savings), float32 for accumulation and LN (numerical stability)
INT8: 4x memory reduction, 2-4x speedup on modern hardware (tensor cores, VNNI)
INT4: 8x memory reduction, enables running large models on consumer GPUs (GPTQ, AWQ)
Key interaction with LayerNorm: LN epsilon must be larger in float16 to avoid numerical issues

mathematical_core.quantize: q = round(w / scale + zero_point), where scale = (w_max - w_min) / (2^bits - 1)
mathematical_core.mixed_precision: Forward/backward in float16, optimizer state and accumulation in float32

mathematical_depth.status: to be populated — quantization error analysis already in PRODUCTION.PRUNING
